files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 3c32782..e161a35 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -189,2 +189,10 @@ Day 182 / Week 26 observations:
 - Cancellations rose to 656, mostly S2 A/C/B. S2 issue age is 1.4 days and social still mentions support delays and output-quality regressions.
 - Week 26 adjustment: start tier-7 R&D, keep ads/capacity/prices stable, increase support/dev again, and post that the Day 182 tax fix is live with credits/validation for affected customers.
+
+Day 189 / Week 27 observations:
+- Tier-5 R&D completed for +0.084 quality. Tier-6 and tier-7 remain in progress.
+- Cash ended at $1.29M after starting tier-7 R&D. Operating subscription payments were $2.03M against about $1.54M in operating costs.
+- S2 content still works at $5k/day, but cancellations jumped to 1,128, led by S2 A (879). This suggests low-tier limits/value and support backlog are now real retention issues.
+- S2 issue age rose to 1.58 days despite heavy support spend. Peak usage was 10.3M/day, still well within tier-5 capacity.
+- Social highlighted rate-limit workflow loss and continued support delays, while some posts praised quality/reliability improvements.
+- Week 27 adjustment: wait one week on tier-8 R&D because cash is just below cost, expand quotas across plans, increase support/dev, keep ads/prices/capacity stable, and post a rate-limit/workflow-continuity response.
